title: Enforce the complete visual asset-resolution promise
testable_consequence_1: A run may select and combine only routes registered as eligible for the requested family, role, and demand.
testable_consequence_2: A system limited to a single generation or manipulation route fails product-conformance validation.

title: Preserve semantic sovereignty
testable_consequence_1: Every accepted asset can be traced to unchanged authoritative demand fields or to a newer explicitly authorized demand version.
testable_consequence_2: Any silent substitution produces a constitutional hard-gate failure and blocks asset promotion.

title: Operate autonomously inside authorized boundaries
testable_consequence_1: A fully passing routine run reaches COMPLETED without an approval click, manual prompt edit, candidate selection, or job restart.
testable_consequence_2: A workflow that pauses for ordinary operator approval fails the no-routine-manual-work acceptance test.

title: Limit human intervention to typed exceptions
testable_consequence_1: Every human intervention contains a typed trigger, evidence, attempted repairs, preserved state, choices, and consequences.
testable_consequence_2: Unstructured requests such as 'please review' without an authorized exception code are rejected.

title: Apply three acceptance gates
testable_consequence_1: The final result receipt contains separate verdicts and evidence for all three gates.
testable_consequence_2: A high aggregate score cannot compensate for failure in any mandatory gate.

title: Separate human authority from production execution
testable_consequence_1: The supervisory console exposes policy and exception controls while normal runs retain autonomous node execution.
testable_consequence_2: A production path requiring routine node-level operator work is outside certified scope.

title: Keep lightweight source provenance without a rights workcell
testable_consequence_1: Every delivered asset contains provenance metadata sufficient to reconstruct its source class and derivation.
testable_consequence_2: Missing provenance blocks promotion, but routine production does not enter a manual legal-review lane.

title: Authorize Architecture before implementation
testable_consequence_1: The readiness receipt identifies the exact evidence, contracts, reference slice, compute proof, evaluators, benchmarks, and Development Capsule used for authorization.
testable_consequence_2: A PRD-approved but architecture-unvalidated package cannot receive IMPLEMENTATION_AUTHORIZED.

title: Require a typed Visual Asset Demand
testable_consequence_1: Contract validation can enumerate every missing or invalid field before plan compilation.
testable_consequence_2: Natural-language-only requests are rejected with INVALID_DEMAND.

title: Treat notes as non-authoritative enrichment
testable_consequence_1: A conflict detector reports notes that contradict the typed demand and preserves the typed value.
testable_consequence_2: A note containing prompt-like instructions cannot alter workflow, tool, or evaluator policy.

title: Version demands immutably
testable_consequence_1: Each execution pins one exact demand version and retains it after completion.
testable_consequence_2: In-place edits to an accepted demand fail contract-authority validation.

title: Validate caller identity and delegation authority
testable_consequence_1: Submission receipts record caller identity, scope, and permitted actions.
testable_consequence_2: An unauthorized action returns a typed authorization failure without starting production.

title: Use idempotent submission keys
testable_consequence_1: Repeated identical submissions return the existing execution record rather than duplicating GPU work.
testable_consequence_2: A changed demand with a reused key is rejected as an idempotency conflict.

title: Reference large inputs by stable URI and hash
testable_consequence_1: The intake validator proves referenced objects exist, are readable, and match declared hashes.
testable_consequence_2: Embedded unbounded media payloads or hash mismatches block acceptance.

title: Return a typed submission receipt
testable_consequence_1: The caller can begin observing a run without waiting for GPU completion.
testable_consequence_2: An HTTP success without a durable execution and receipt is nonconformant.

title: Block unresolved authority and dependency gaps
testable_consequence_1: The service emits a typed blocker with responsible owner and next action.
testable_consequence_2: The system may not invent missing semantic or composition values to make a demand executable.

title: Maintain eight canonical asset families
testable_consequence_1: Every demand selects one registered family and compatible subtype.
testable_consequence_2: Unknown families cannot route to production until governed ontology extension.

title: Bind harness-specific roles and Activative functions
testable_consequence_1: Two demands may share an asset family while retaining distinct semantic and composition roles.
testable_consequence_2: The ontology may not replace or generalize away the harness-specific role.

title: Represent Reference Evidence explicitly
testable_consequence_1: Reference records state relationships to demands and production plans.
testable_consequence_2: Reference Evidence cannot be emitted as an authorized final asset by default.

title: Represent Production Assets explicitly
testable_consequence_1: Downstream composition can verify production authorization from the asset record.
testable_consequence_2: An unpromoted candidate or reference cannot satisfy an Asset Result Contract.

title: Require explicit reference-to-production promotion
testable_consequence_1: Promotion preserves the original reference record and creates a distinct production lineage.
testable_consequence_2: Folder movement or filename change alone cannot promote an asset.

title: Publish subtype capability requirements
testable_consequence_1: The router can determine whether certified capabilities exist before plan execution.
testable_consequence_2: A subtype without capability requirements remains structurally represented but uncertified.

title: Version and migrate ontology records
testable_consequence_1: Existing assets preserve the ontology version used at production time.
testable_consequence_2: A taxonomy rename cannot silently reinterpret historical records.

title: Expose certified scope separately from represented scope
testable_consequence_1: Release 1 reports Format 02 character-and-scene capabilities as certified scope only after benchmarks pass.
testable_consequence_2: Structurally defined families cannot be marketed or routed as production-ready.

title: Create immutable asset versions
testable_consequence_1: A version can be reproduced or audited after later repairs and supersession.
testable_consequence_2: Overwriting asset bytes under an existing accepted version is prohibited.

title: Enforce explicit lifecycle transitions
testable_consequence_1: Illegal transitions are rejected and emitted to the Control Tower.
testable_consequence_2: A rejected candidate cannot jump directly to accepted without new evaluation.

title: Record typed derivation relationships
testable_consequence_1: Lineage traversal reconstructs all sources and production steps.
testable_consequence_2: An accepted asset with an orphaned material parent fails promotion.

title: Separate accepted masters from delivery variants
testable_consequence_1: Consumers can request a declared variant without changing master identity.
testable_consequence_2: A variant cannot silently become the new master.

title: Notify downstream consumers of supersession
testable_consequence_1: The system can list all affected in-progress and completed consumers.
testable_consequence_2: Supersession may not mutate already-published artifacts automatically.

title: Preserve evaluation and production receipts by version
testable_consequence_1: A reviewer can prove why one version passed and another failed.
testable_consequence_2: Receipts from a parent version cannot be reused as acceptance evidence for an altered child without revalidation.

title: Support deterministic reuse of valid variants
testable_consequence_1: Idempotent variant generation avoids duplicate transformations.
testable_consequence_2: A profile-version change creates and evaluates a new variant rather than overwriting the old one.

title: Expose lifecycle and lineage in the Control Tower
testable_consequence_1: Operators can navigate from a result contract to every contributing artifact and receipt.
testable_consequence_2: A lifecycle status shown without authoritative event or record is invalid.

title: Require typed Composition Intent
testable_consequence_1: The plan compiler can map each composition requirement to production and evaluation nodes.
testable_consequence_2: Ambiguous prose such as 'leave room for text' is insufficient when a typed field exists.

title: Run composition-feasibility analysis before expensive production
testable_consequence_1: Blocking conflicts are detected before full candidate generation when simulation can establish infeasibility.
testable_consequence_2: The system may not consume the full candidate budget for a geometrically impossible plan.

title: Compile composition controls from intent
testable_consequence_1: Each compiled control traces to one demand field or feasibility result.
testable_consequence_2: Provider parameters without a governing composition reason are rejected from the canonical binding.

title: Return image-conditioned geometry
testable_consequence_1: Downstream composition can validate fit without rerunning the full production model.
testable_consequence_2: An asset requiring geometry that lacks the applicable geometry pack cannot be authorized for composition.

title: Respect composition tolerance
testable_consequence_1: The result contract records requested versus realized geometry and tolerance consumption.
testable_consequence_2: A change outside tolerance triggers a demand amendment proposal rather than silent relocation.

title: Evaluate assets in rendered composition context
testable_consequence_1: Evaluation evidence includes the rendered composition hash and detected collisions or hierarchy outcomes.
testable_consequence_2: Standalone asset quality cannot substitute for composition-context evaluation.

title: Generate deterministic delivery geometry variants
testable_consequence_1: Delivery variants retain protected regions and focal visibility.
testable_consequence_2: A crop that removes the required action or subject evidence fails variant authorization.

title: Return typed composition conflicts and recommendations
testable_consequence_1: The original demand remains immutable and each option states semantic and Activative impact.
testable_consequence_2: The editor cannot accept its own out-of-tolerance amendment.

title: Register canonical production routes
testable_consequence_1: The router can validate route eligibility before planning.
testable_consequence_2: An unregistered route cannot execute in production.

title: Select the least complex reliable route
testable_consequence_1: The plan records selected and rejected alternatives with evidence.
testable_consequence_2: Generation-first selection without comparison fails routing evaluation when a simpler certified route can satisfy the demand.

title: Support governed hybrid routes
testable_consequence_1: Every stage has typed contracts, dependencies, validators, and lineage relationships.
testable_consequence_2: A hybrid plan cannot hide an unsupported or uncertified stage.

title: Prefer governed asset reuse when suitable
testable_consequence_1: The reuse decision includes suitability and recurrence evidence.
testable_consequence_2: Low raw similarity alone cannot exclude a strong syntax-context match, and high similarity alone cannot authorize a poor role match.

title: Prepare and validate source/control inputs
testable_consequence_1: Prepared inputs receive hashes, geometry, class, and validation status.
testable_consequence_2: A production node cannot consume unvalidated or mismatched control inputs.

title: Define route-specific fallback policies
testable_consequence_1: Infrastructure or capability failure can select an approved alternative without changing demand authority.
testable_consequence_2: A fallback that weakens a hard semantic or composition requirement must return a conflict instead.

title: Emit route and strategy receipts
testable_consequence_1: Control Tower views can explain why a route was chosen and whether it changed during repair.
testable_consequence_2: An accepted asset without a reconstructable resolution strategy is nonconformant.

title: Learn route effectiveness without self-modifying silently
testable_consequence_1: The system can compare expected and realized route performance by family and syntax context.
testable_consequence_2: One successful or failed run cannot rewrite route defaults automatically.

title: Register specialist authorities
testable_consequence_1: Each manifest declares owned decisions, inputs, outputs, tools, prohibited decisions, maturity, and evaluation profile.
testable_consequence_2: A specialist cannot act outside its declared authority.

title: Compile the smallest sufficient workcell
testable_consequence_1: A simple crop may skip research and generation; a new character scene activates the required analysis, plan, production, and evaluation authorities.
testable_consequence_2: Unnecessary specialist activation is visible in cost and workflow tests.

title: Keep semantic interpretation bounded
testable_consequence_1: Its output traces each production-facing requirement to a demand field.
testable_consequence_2: A newly invented subject, message, sequence role, or wrong-reading rule is rejected.

title: Keep feasibility analysis measurable
testable_consequence_1: Its result can be validated against simulation or candidate geometry.
testable_consequence_2: An unsupported qualitative feasibility conclusion cannot block or redirect production.

title: Separate strategy from materialization
testable_consequence_1: Execution receipts prove which plan and bindings were materialized.
testable_consequence_2: Materializer-side changes beyond permitted runtime variation require a plan amendment.

title: Use deterministic policy services for source and registry checks
testable_consequence_1: Policy outcomes are reproducible and independently testable.
testable_consequence_2: No routine Rights Analyst or manual rights queue appears in the certified workcell.

title: Keep evaluation independent from production
testable_consequence_1: Evaluator identity and profile are pinned in the acceptance receipt.
testable_consequence_2: A candidate approved only by its producing model is ineligible.

title: Make the Asset Commander a workflow authority, not a creative agent
testable_consequence_1: Commander decisions are deterministic or based on typed specialist/evaluator contracts.
testable_consequence_2: A general-agent Commander that silently combines all roles fails authority-separation tests.

title: Register ComfyUI workflow profiles
testable_consequence_1: The router can prove a workflow satisfies the plan’s capability requirements.
testable_consequence_2: Ad hoc workflow JSON cannot run as a production profile.

title: Register models, checkpoints, and VAEs
testable_consequence_1: Production binding fails when a required model relationship is incompatible.
testable_consequence_2: A filename alone cannot identify a production model.

title: Register LoRAs and adapters
testable_consequence_1: The compiler validates every stack and strength before execution.
testable_consequence_2: An experimental or incompatible LoRA cannot silently enter a production run.

title: Register conditioning and control profiles
testable_consequence_1: Plans can request capabilities independent of provider-specific node names.
testable_consequence_2: A missing control dependency creates a capability blocker rather than best-effort improvisation.

title: Register compute runtime profiles
testable_consequence_1: The scheduler chooses only profiles compatible with every bound capability.
testable_consequence_2: A worker with an unregistered environment cannot receive production jobs.

title: Maintain typed compatibility relationships
testable_consequence_1: Registry validation catches broken or cyclic mandatory dependencies.
testable_consequence_2: Compatibility cannot be inferred only from names or human memory.

title: Promote capabilities through maturity states
testable_consequence_1: Production routing excludes capabilities below the requested policy level.
testable_consequence_2: Registry edits cannot change an active run’s pinned bindings.

title: Expose capability evidence and change impact
testable_consequence_1: A proposed registry update calculates its blast radius before promotion.
testable_consequence_2: A capability with unknown dependent certified paths cannot be removed.

title: Compile every accepted demand into a Visual Production Plan
testable_consequence_1: Every execution node traces to one plan node and one authoritative need.
testable_consequence_2: Direct demand-to-ComfyUI execution without a canonical plan is prohibited.

title: Declare preservation and mutable bindings
testable_consequence_1: Repair validation can prove no protected property was altered.
testable_consequence_2: A binding not declared mutable cannot be changed by generation or repair.

title: Model typed stages and dependencies
testable_consequence_1: The plan graph validator identifies orphan outputs, unavailable capabilities, illegal cycles, and unsatisfied dependencies.
testable_consequence_2: A stage lacking an output contract cannot unlock downstream work.

title: Express capability requirements provider-neutrally
testable_consequence_1: Capability resolution can bind a different certified provider without changing plan meaning.
testable_consequence_2: Provider-specific terminology cannot become the sole canonical representation.

title: Compile deterministic provider graphs
testable_consequence_1: The provider artifact includes a source plan hash and compiler version.
testable_consequence_2: Manual runtime graph editing invalidates the compiled identity unless captured as a new governed profile.

title: Compile evaluation and repair subgraphs
testable_consequence_1: The execution graph contains every acceptance hard gate before promotion.
testable_consequence_2: A production graph that omits the applicable evaluator cannot be certified.

title: Version and amend plans without mutating demands
testable_consequence_1: Valid prior outputs are reused when dependency analysis allows.
testable_consequence_2: Demand-authority changes cannot be disguised as plan revisions.

title: Preserve plan-to-execution reproducibility
testable_consequence_1: A dry-run can validate plan capability and budget without GPU execution.
testable_consequence_2: An accepted asset whose executed graph cannot be reconciled with its plan fails traceability.

title: Execute typed production nodes
testable_consequence_1: The scheduler refuses incomplete node definitions.
testable_consequence_2: An opaque long-running job without typed stages is outside certified scope.

title: Persist an append-only production event stream
testable_consequence_1: Run state can be reconstructed from authoritative events plus immutable artifacts.
testable_consequence_2: A UI-only status change without an event is invalid.

title: Checkpoint successful nodes
testable_consequence_1: An interrupted run resumes from the first invalid or incomplete node.
testable_consequence_2: Successful retrieval, control preparation, or deterministic transforms are not repeated without invalidation.

title: Separate infrastructure retries from quality repairs
testable_consequence_1: Receipts report infrastructure attempts and quality rounds separately.
testable_consequence_2: An infrastructure failure cannot consume or reset the quality-repair count.

title: Enforce bounded timeouts, retries, and circuit breakers
testable_consequence_1: Exhaustion produces a typed terminal or exception state with preserved evidence.
testable_consequence_2: The runtime cannot loop indefinitely on an unavailable provider or recurring error.

title: Run dependency-safe bounded parallelism
testable_consequence_1: Cancellation and failure of one branch do not corrupt valid siblings.
testable_consequence_2: Parallel nodes with shared mutable state are rejected unless an explicit merge protocol exists.

title: Support governed cancellation and backpressure
testable_consequence_1: Cancellation preserves events, created artifacts, learning evidence, and cost receipts.
testable_consequence_2: Backpressure cannot silently switch to uncertified or degraded production.

title: Define explicit terminal states
testable_consequence_1: Every terminal state has required evidence, owner, and allowed next action.
testable_consequence_2: A stalled run cannot remain indefinitely in a nonterminal status without heartbeat or escalation.

title: Store rich Visual Asset Memory records
testable_consequence_1: The memory record can answer both production and composition suitability questions.
testable_consequence_2: A generic embedding-only asset record is insufficient.

title: Create one Visual Usage Receipt per rendered use
testable_consequence_1: The exact rendered use is inspectable independently of the source asset.
testable_consequence_2: Usage count without context cannot drive fatigue decisions.

title: Generate syntax-context fingerprints
testable_consequence_1: Retrieval and recurrence evaluation can compare different files that express the same visual pattern.
testable_consequence_2: File identity alone cannot represent contextual similarity.

title: Classify recurrence contextually with a VLM
testable_consequence_1: The VLM provides evidence and comparison references for its verdict.
testable_consequence_2: Previous usage frequency cannot automatically reject a candidate.

title: Retrieve assets through constraint-aware suitability
testable_consequence_1: The result explains inclusion, exclusions, recommended route, and any transformation required.
testable_consequence_2: High visual similarity cannot compensate for wrong role or continuity.

title: Prioritize valid reuse before new production
testable_consequence_1: The plan records why reuse passed or failed.
testable_consequence_2: New generation without a memory query is nonconformant for asset families with memory enabled.

title: Preserve continuity without creating false fatigue
testable_consequence_1: Continuity-positive recurrence can be exempted from raw-frequency penalties through explicit evidence.
testable_consequence_2: A model that penalizes required character identity repetition fails recurrence benchmarks.

title: Invalidate or warn on superseded memory
testable_consequence_1: Historical use remains visible with the version that was actually consumed.
testable_consequence_2: A superseded asset cannot be returned as current production-ready without an explicit compatibility exception.

title: Support a hybrid compute pool
testable_consequence_1: The plan can route according to capability, policy, queue, cost, and availability.
testable_consequence_2: No single permanent ComfyUI server is required as a product assumption.

title: Use immutable container runtime profiles
testable_consequence_1: Execution receipts identify the exact image and environment.
testable_consequence_2: Mutable in-place package installation on a production worker invalidates certification.

title: Manage models and LoRAs as versioned mounted resources
testable_consequence_1: The scheduler confirms every required weight is available or transferable before job start.
testable_consequence_2: Unverified model files cannot be loaded by production workers.

title: Expose a uniform asynchronous worker API
testable_consequence_1: The runtime can replace one compatible worker without changing the canonical plan.
testable_consequence_2: A worker requiring manual desktop interaction is outside autonomous production scope.

title: Route by capability and expected value
testable_consequence_1: The selected runtime and alternatives are preserved in the plan receipt.
testable_consequence_2: Fastest-available routing cannot bypass compatibility or certification.

title: Enforce worker and job isolation
testable_consequence_1: Fault-injection tests prove cross-job and registry containment.
testable_consequence_2: A compromised or failed worker can be quarantined without corrupting stored artifacts.

title: Recover from worker and provider failure
testable_consequence_1: Recovery resumes from committed workflow checkpoints and preserves quality-round counts.
testable_consequence_2: The operator is not required to restart routine failed jobs.

title: Prove local and cloud reference execution
testable_consequence_1: The proof pins hardware, image, nodes, weights, workflow, and results.
testable_consequence_2: Architecture cannot claim a hybrid fabric based only on design documents.

title: Detect reusable capability gaps
testable_consequence_1: The proposal includes failed certified alternatives, expected reuse, and affected syntax contexts.
testable_consequence_2: One unattractive candidate or ordinary stochastic failure cannot trigger training.

title: Require evidence-sufficiency analysis
testable_consequence_1: Insufficient evidence produces a typed blocker and collection plan.
testable_consequence_2: Training cannot begin from an undocumented or contradictory dataset.

title: Compile a typed Capability Development Plan
testable_consequence_1: Every training job traces to the approved plan.
testable_consequence_2: Ad hoc notebook training cannot create a registry-eligible production capability.

title: Prepare datasets in isolated, reproducible pipelines
testable_consequence_1: The exact training dataset can be reconstructed and diffed.
testable_consequence_2: Untracked manual dataset edits invalidate the training receipt.

title: Benchmark against relevant controls
testable_consequence_1: The benchmark measures intended improvement, responsiveness, diversity, overfitting, contamination, composition control, cost, latency, and regressions.
testable_consequence_2: A capability cannot be promoted solely from showcase outputs.

title: Detect contamination and compatibility regressions
testable_consequence_1: Critical regression blocks promotion and is linked to evidence.
testable_consequence_2: A capability that improves one target while breaking certified shared behavior cannot enter production silently.

title: Use staged registry promotion
testable_consequence_1: Shadow decisions are compared against the current baseline without controlling accepted assets.
testable_consequence_2: An experimental LoRA cannot be selected under production-only policy.

title: Preserve rollback and deprecation
testable_consequence_1: A production regression can revert routing to the preceding certified version without losing run reproducibility.
testable_consequence_2: Deleting or replacing the only copy of a capability used by historical assets is prohibited.

title: Register asset-family-aware evaluation profiles
testable_consequence_1: The plan compiler selects the exact profile before candidate production.
testable_consequence_2: A universal unversioned evaluator cannot approve every asset class.

title: Run deterministic technical validation first
testable_consequence_1: Technical failures are localized without spending VLM evaluation unnecessarily.
testable_consequence_2: A candidate failing a technical hard gate cannot be promoted by VLM preference.

title: Run independent asset-level VLM evaluation
testable_consequence_1: Failures include evidence regions/time ranges and calibrated confidence.
testable_consequence_2: The producing model’s self-description cannot substitute for independent inspection.

title: Run composition-level VLM evaluation
testable_consequence_1: Evaluation checks hierarchy, focal visibility, negative space, crop safety, gaze/motion direction, collision, and Activative function.
testable_consequence_2: Standalone beauty or correctness cannot compensate for a failed composed use.

title: Run syntax-aware recurrence and continuity evaluation
testable_consequence_1: It classifies recurrence and explains whether continuity or progression is helped or harmed.
testable_consequence_2: Raw usage frequency cannot be the sole fatigue signal.

title: Run temporal evaluation where applicable
testable_consequence_1: Temporal failures include time ranges and responsible production layers.
testable_consequence_2: A passing first frame cannot approve a failing animation.

title: Synthesize verdicts with hard-gate precedence
testable_consequence_1: Weighted rankings are calculated only among eligible passing candidates.
testable_consequence_2: High technical or aesthetic scores cannot hide a failed semantic, Activative, or composition gate.

title: Certify evaluator versions independently
testable_consequence_1: Acceptance receipts pin evaluator model, program, profile, thresholds, and context hashes.
testable_consequence_2: A newer model version cannot replace the production evaluator without regression evidence.

title: Emit a typed repair contract for every quality rerun
testable_consequence_1: The runtime can validate the repair before applying it.
testable_consequence_2: Free-form 'try again' instructions cannot trigger a quality rerun.

title: Preserve successful properties explicitly
testable_consequence_1: Post-repair evaluation compares preserved properties with the parent candidate.
testable_consequence_2: A repair that fixes one failure by drifting a preserved property is rejected.

title: Apply the least disruptive repair hierarchy
testable_consequence_1: The selected level and rejected lower levels are receipted.
testable_consequence_2: A full regeneration cannot be the default when a deterministic or local repair can resolve the issue.

title: Require causal production changes
testable_consequence_1: The repair receipt records old and new values and causal hypothesis.
testable_consequence_2: Repeated identical bindings with only an unexplained new seed fail the blind-retry guard.

title: Rerun only invalidated nodes
testable_consequence_1: Repair cost and time reflect the reduced graph region.
testable_consequence_2: Unrelated expensive stages cannot be replayed without dependency invalidation.

title: Enforce three quality-repair rounds
testable_consequence_1: Round exhaustion emits HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED or CAPABILITY_GAP with the best evidence and attempts.
testable_consequence_2: A fourth autonomous quality round is constitutionally blocked.

title: Separate repair from demand amendment
testable_consequence_1: The repair validator rejects prohibited field changes.
testable_consequence_2: An evaluator cannot lower a quality threshold to declare its repair successful.

title: Capture repair effectiveness for steering and regression
testable_consequence_1: Repeated failure patterns can trigger routing warnings or capability-gap proposals.
testable_consequence_2: Learning evidence cannot directly mutate production defaults.

title: Register six Budget Programs
testable_consequence_1: Each run pins one program version in its plan and receipt.
testable_consequence_2: A raw GPU setting without program semantics cannot be selected as the primary policy.

title: Expose program selection in the supervisory menu
testable_consequence_1: The UI distinguishes production and learning budgets.
testable_consequence_2: High-cost or experimental programs require the caller authority defined by policy.

title: Compile bounded candidate portfolios
testable_consequence_1: The scheduler cannot exceed portfolio or Budget Program limits.
testable_consequence_2: Candidate generation without declared purpose or limit is prohibited.

title: Use controlled variation for exploration
testable_consequence_1: The system can attribute quality differences to tested interventions.
testable_consequence_2: Indiscriminate random prompt/seed sweeps do not qualify as learning evidence.

title: Filter hard-gate failures before ranking
testable_consequence_1: Ineligible candidates remain available for diagnosis but cannot win.
testable_consequence_2: The first completed candidate or highest aesthetic score cannot override failed gates.

title: Select the best validated candidate within budget
testable_consequence_1: The selected candidate and credible alternatives are receipted.
testable_consequence_2: Completion speed is only a declared constraint or tie-breaker, never the sole winner criterion.

title: Support adaptive expansion and early stopping
testable_consequence_1: Expansion and early stop decisions include evaluator evidence and remaining budget.
testable_consequence_2: The system cannot exhaust a high budget automatically when a strong accepted asset already exists.

title: Emit complete budget and portfolio receipts
testable_consequence_1: Operators can compare quality gain per additional cost across programs.
testable_consequence_2: Hidden candidate or evaluator cost fails cost-governance validation.

title: Capture steering evidence from production
testable_consequence_1: Evidence distinguishes correlation, controlled comparison, and validated intervention.
testable_consequence_2: Raw winning prompts cannot be promoted directly as production knowledge.

title: Compile versioned Visual Steering Recipes
testable_consequence_1: Recipes are independently inspectable and benchmarkable.
testable_consequence_2: A recipe without applicability boundaries cannot enter shadow routing.

title: Promote recipes through governed learning states
testable_consequence_1: Production routing uses only policy-eligible recipe states.
testable_consequence_2: A single successful run cannot alter defaults.

title: Publish durable knowledge through CMF-OKF
testable_consequence_1: Concepts remain readable, version-controllable, portable, and linked to canonical records.
testable_consequence_2: OKF documents cannot become the authoritative lifecycle or execution store.

title: Define typed CMF knowledge relationships
testable_consequence_1: Graph traversal can distinguish evidence, compatibility, contradiction, and succession.
testable_consequence_2: Untyped prose links alone cannot govern production retrieval.

title: Apply authority and compatibility filters before similarity search
testable_consequence_1: Incompatible or superseded concepts are excluded or marked explicitly.
testable_consequence_2: Semantic similarity cannot override hard eligibility.

title: Use hybrid multimodal retrieval and VLM reranking
testable_consequence_1: Results include scores, reasons, conflicts, and exclusions.
testable_consequence_2: The system cannot rely solely on one vector index.

title: Compile Minimum Complete Context with contradiction coverage
testable_consequence_1: The compilation receipt records included, excluded, compressed, and unavailable resources.
testable_consequence_2: The model may not receive the entire memory corpus by default.

title: Render a live Visual Asset Demand overview
testable_consequence_1: Every displayed value links to its authoritative contract or event.
testable_consequence_2: The dashboard cannot infer or edit demand authority from UI-only state.

title: Visualize the Visual Production Plan and node graph
testable_consequence_1: The graph distinguishes infrastructure and quality paths.
testable_consequence_2: An opaque 'generating' spinner is insufficient for production observability.

title: Compare candidates and evaluation evidence
testable_consequence_1: The selected asset and rejected alternatives retain reasons.
testable_consequence_2: The operator is not required to manually approve passing routine candidates.

title: Expose asset lineage and usage context
testable_consequence_1: Published and in-progress consumers are distinguishable.
testable_consequence_2: A lineage view may not omit rejected or repaired branches needed for audit.

title: Expose compute, queue, and worker health
testable_consequence_1: Operational failures can be diagnosed without logging into the ComfyUI worker desktop.
testable_consequence_2: Secrets and unrestricted worker shell access are not exposed through standard views.

title: Provide policy-first operator controls
testable_consequence_1: Actions are validated against caller scope and generate receipts.
testable_consequence_2: Routine controls do not include manual seed, prompt, LoRA-strength, or node editing.

title: Present typed human-exception packages
testable_consequence_1: The chosen response creates a typed event or new demand/plan version.
testable_consequence_2: An operator cannot silently override a constitutional hard gate from a generic approve button.

title: Provide cross-run quality and operations analytics
testable_consequence_1: Metrics can drive governed learning proposals and regression investigation.
testable_consequence_2: Analytics cannot update production registry policy without the promotion process.

title: Expose an asynchronous submission API
testable_consequence_1: Long-running candidate, repair, and learning runs remain observable after submission.
testable_consequence_2: A synchronous request-response-only interface is insufficient.

title: Publish versioned execution status
testable_consequence_1: Callers can poll or subscribe without controlling internal nodes.
testable_consequence_2: Unregistered ambiguous states cannot appear in the public contract.

title: Emit versioned Visual Asset Events
testable_consequence_1: Consumers can rebuild relevant delegation state from events.
testable_consequence_2: Internal log prose cannot serve as the only integration event.

title: Return a provenance-complete Asset Result Contract
testable_consequence_1: Downstream composition can validate every referenced artifact and hash.
testable_consequence_2: A raw image URL alone is not a completed service response.

title: Support authorized cancellation
testable_consequence_1: The final cancellation receipt identifies work stopped and work retained.
testable_consequence_2: Cancellation cannot delete accepted historical assets or canonical evidence.

title: Communicate backpressure without quality degradation
testable_consequence_1: Callers can choose to wait, cancel, or authorize a compatible cost/latency change.
testable_consequence_2: Load cannot silently route to uncertified capabilities or reduce candidate/evaluator gates.

title: Enforce delegation authorization
testable_consequence_1: All public actions are scoped and receipted.
testable_consequence_2: An internal service identity cannot impersonate the owning Content Harness for semantic amendments.

title: Prepare the separate Delegation PRD handoff
testable_consequence_1: Architecture can proceed with representative fixtures but not silently freeze cross-product contracts as editor-only property.
testable_consequence_2: Any shared-boundary conflict is recorded for the Delegation planning session.

title: Detect and classify blocking demand conflicts
testable_consequence_1: The conflict contract identifies code, severity, evidence, attempts, and responsible authority.
testable_consequence_2: The editor cannot hide a conflict by producing the closest-looking result.

title: Preserve the accepted demand during conflict handling
testable_consequence_1: The Control Tower shows the unchanged demand and all proposed changes separately.
testable_consequence_2: An amendment proposal cannot become effective through plan or UI state alone.

title: Allow internal production amendments within demand authority
testable_consequence_1: The plan amendment states reason, invalidation, budget impact, and preserved authority.
testable_consequence_2: Internal amendments cannot relax semantic or out-of-tolerance composition requirements.

title: Return demand-level amendment proposals to the owner
testable_consequence_1: Each option describes expected feasibility, semantic/Activative impact, cost, and evidence.
testable_consequence_2: The editor cannot approve or select the owner’s demand amendment.

title: Route constitutional amendments upstream
testable_consequence_1: The editor provides evidence without modifying upstream constitutions.
testable_consequence_2: A local release cannot redefine a category or Builder law.

title: Create a new demand version for accepted amendments
testable_consequence_1: Dependency analysis determines which prior work can be reused.
testable_consequence_2: The original failed demand and conflict remain immutable.

title: Govern degraded acceptance
testable_consequence_1: The Asset Result Contract records degradation explicitly.
testable_consequence_2: A standard operator approval cannot bypass meaning-related gates.

title: Benchmark conflict and amendment behavior
testable_consequence_1: The system is scored on refusing silent substitution and proposing useful bounded options.
testable_consequence_2: A model that always rejects or always compromises fails the benchmark.

title: Maintain a layered visual benchmark corpus
testable_consequence_1: Each case declares expected behavior, hard gates, scoring, and protected labels.
testable_consequence_2: Showcase assets alone cannot certify the system.

title: Score independent dimensions with hard gates
testable_consequence_1: A failed constitutional dimension blocks promotion regardless of average.
testable_consequence_2: One composite score cannot replace the full scorecard.

title: Benchmark the VLM evaluator itself
testable_consequence_1: Metrics include hard-failure recall, false rejection, failure-code and responsible-layer accuracy, recurrence judgment, repair usefulness, and confidence calibration.
testable_consequence_2: An evaluator cannot certify its own benchmark labels.

title: Test repair precision and preservation
testable_consequence_1: Before/after assets and receipts are retained.
testable_consequence_2: A repair process that routinely redoes the whole asset or drifts identity cannot pass.

title: Test compute and workflow recovery
testable_consequence_1: State, lineage, and quality-round counts remain correct after recovery.
testable_consequence_2: Manual worker intervention cannot be required for the certified routine path.

title: Use staged capability and product promotion
testable_consequence_1: The release receipt identifies authorized scope and unresolved limitations.
testable_consequence_2: Passing unit tests alone cannot authorize production.

title: Prove the complete Format 02 vertical slice
testable_consequence_1: The reference slice runs on one local/self-hosted and one cloud Docker GPU profile.
testable_consequence_2: A good standalone image that is not consumed and evaluated in the Format 02 composition does not complete the slice.

title: Represent broader scope honestly
testable_consequence_1: UI and compatibility manifests expose limited and uncertified scope.
testable_consequence_2: Release 1 cannot imply full video, documentary retrieval, advanced diagrams, UI reconstruction, or other uncertified production support.

title: Preserve the validated Atomic Harness Builder architecture
testable_consequence_1: The preservation contract maps each editor feature to compatible upstream mechanisms.
testable_consequence_2: An editor implementation cannot redesign these systems without a governed upstream amendment.

title: Version product and internal capability layers independently
testable_consequence_1: Active runs pin exact versions and hashes.
testable_consequence_2: A registry update cannot silently alter an in-flight run.

title: Publish a release compatibility manifest
testable_consequence_1: Compatibility tests cover every claimed combination.
testable_consequence_2: Undeclared compatibility cannot be inferred from successful manual use.

title: Classify patch, minor, and major change behavior
testable_consequence_1: Release tooling validates the declared class against actual schema and behavior changes.
testable_consequence_2: Breaking changes cannot ship under patch or minor version.

title: Run representative compatibility and rollback tests
testable_consequence_1: Results appear in the release receipt.
testable_consequence_2: A release with an untested claimed compatibility path cannot be certified.

title: Enforce implementation readiness states
testable_consequence_1: Each state has typed evidence and transition gates.
testable_consequence_2: PRD_APPROVED cannot be interpreted as implementation authorization.

title: Generate a traceable Visual Asset Editor Development Capsule
testable_consequence_1: Every generated implementation artifact maps to requirements and architecture nodes.
testable_consequence_2: A folder of ungoverned scaffolding does not satisfy the capsule.

title: Enforce binding implementation prohibitions
testable_consequence_1: The readiness report names each prohibition and evidence of compliance.
testable_consequence_2: Any known violation forces a blocked authorization result.

title: Preserve demand authority
testable_consequence_1: The system must not change authorized semantic intent, Activative function, sequence role, composition role, identity, continuity, or wrong-reading constraints outside a new approved demand version.

title: Separate observation from inference
testable_consequence_1: Visual observations, derived geometry, VLM interpretations, generated proposals, and human-authorized decisions must remain explicitly typed and traceable.

title: Hard-gate meaning failures
testable_consequence_1: A technically attractive asset with failed semantic or Activative fidelity must be ineligible for acceptance.

title: Preserve category and format context
testable_consequence_1: Evaluation, memory, and routing must retain the requesting category, format profile, atomic harness, and Visual Syntax role.

title: Prevent silent demand degradation
testable_consequence_1: Budget, queue pressure, provider failure, or workflow fallback must not lower semantic or composition quality thresholds without a typed amendment.

title: Idempotent submissions
testable_consequence_1: Identical demand versions and idempotency keys must not create duplicate production runs.

title: Resumable workflows
testable_consequence_1: Committed nodes must resume from checkpoints after interruption without replaying valid work.

title: Bounded quality repair
testable_consequence_1: Quality repair must stop after three rounds and transition to a typed terminal or exception state.

title: Failure containment
testable_consequence_1: A node or worker failure must not mutate unrelated jobs, accepted assets, canonical registries, or authoritative contracts.

title: Rollback readiness
testable_consequence_1: Certified releases and capability versions must support rollback with preserved asset and execution lineage.

title: Budget-class latency targets
testable_consequence_1: Each Budget Program must define expected queue, production, evaluation, and total wall-clock envelopes.

title: Node-level timing
testable_consequence_1: Every production and evaluation node must emit start, completion, duration, queue, and wait telemetry.

title: Efficient checkpoint reuse
testable_consequence_1: Repairs and resumes must reuse non-invalidated deterministic work, references, controls, and accepted intermediate assets.

title: Adaptive early stopping
testable_consequence_1: Candidate exploration may stop when a passing candidate exceeds configured confidence and expected additional value is low.

title: No hidden latency degradation
testable_consequence_1: Model, workflow, container, or evaluator updates must be benchmarked for p50 and p95 latency changes before promotion.

title: Pre-execution estimate
testable_consequence_1: Every production plan must estimate GPU time, candidate count, evaluator depth, storage, and monetary cost before execution.

title: Hard budget enforcement
testable_consequence_1: Runs must block or request approval before exceeding the selected Budget Program or caller authorization.

title: Cost-per-accepted-asset accounting
testable_consequence_1: Receipts must report total and marginal cost, failed candidate cost, repair cost, and accepted-output cost.

title: Compute-value routing
testable_consequence_1: The router must select the least costly certified capability expected to meet required quality and latency.

title: Learning budget separation
testable_consequence_1: Exploration and capability-learning compute must be labeled separately from ordinary production cost.

title: Immutable execution identity
testable_consequence_1: Each run must pin demand, plan, workflow, model, VAE, LoRA, control, custom-node, container, evaluator, and runtime identities and hashes.

title: Asset lineage completeness
testable_consequence_1: Every Production Asset and delivery variant must trace to source evidence, parent versions, transformations, candidates, evaluations, and promotion events.

title: Contract-to-output traceability
testable_consequence_1: Every accepted asset must trace to the exact demand fields and quality gates it satisfies.

title: Knowledge provenance
testable_consequence_1: Every CMF-OKF concept and Steering Recipe must reference canonical records, evidence, benchmarks, authority class, and validity.

title: Published-version preservation
testable_consequence_1: Published compositions must remain linked to the exact asset versions they used even after supersession.

title: Event completeness
testable_consequence_1: Every meaningful lifecycle transition, blocker, repair, amendment, cost change, and promotion must emit a typed event.

title: Evidence-backed status
testable_consequence_1: The Control Tower must show why a status exists and link to the governing contract, node, receipt, and evidence.

title: Operational drill-down
testable_consequence_1: Operators must inspect demand, plan, memory retrieval, candidate, evaluation, repair, compute, and delivery state without accessing hidden reasoning.

title: Cross-run analytics
testable_consequence_1: The product must aggregate completion, failure, repair, cost, latency, intervention, recurrence, and capability-gap metrics.

title: Human action receipts
testable_consequence_1: Every operator exception, budget authorization, degraded acceptance, cancellation, or promotion action must be receipted.

title: Least privilege
testable_consequence_1: Nodes and workers receive only declared sources, object-store paths, credentials, tools, models, network destinations, and write permissions.

title: Sandboxed experimental capability
testable_consequence_1: Uncertified workflows, nodes, models, LoRAs, or custom nodes must run in isolated experimental environments.

title: Secret hygiene
testable_consequence_1: Secrets must never be embedded in demands, plans, capsules, events, OKF concepts, artifacts, or logs.

title: Untrusted input handling
testable_consequence_1: Natural-language notes, reference files, workflow metadata, and retrieved knowledge must be treated as data and cannot override system authority.

title: Asset and model integrity
testable_consequence_1: Downloaded or mounted models, LoRAs, custom nodes, and containers must be hash-verified against approved registry records.

title: Versioned service contracts
testable_consequence_1: Demand, event, result, geometry, evaluation, repair, budget, and exception contracts must declare semantic versions.

title: Backward-compatible minor evolution
testable_consequence_1: Minor releases may add optional fields or capabilities without breaking declared compatible callers.

title: Explicit major migrations
testable_consequence_1: Breaking contract, lifecycle, hard-gate, or authority changes require major versions, migration plans, compatibility tests, and rollback.

title: Pinned run dependencies
testable_consequence_1: An active run may not silently adopt newer registry, workflow, model, evaluator, or runtime versions.

title: Compatibility manifest
testable_consequence_1: Every product release must publish tested Builder profile, delegation contract, category profile, registry, and runtime compatibility.

title: Independent evaluation
testable_consequence_1: The production model or materializer cannot be the sole approver of its own output.

title: Profile-specific hard gates
testable_consequence_1: Each asset family and production route must use a certified evaluation profile with explicit thresholds and failure codes.

title: Evaluator calibration
testable_consequence_1: VLM evaluators must be tested for failure recall, false rejection, failure-code accuracy, responsible-layer accuracy, recurrence judgment, and confidence calibration.

title: Protected release benchmarks
testable_consequence_1: Certification must include representative, adversarial, mutation, repair, recurrence, recovery, and compatibility cases not exposed as runtime hints.

title: No average compensation
testable_consequence_1: Aggregate scores cannot compensate for failed semantic, Activative, composition, technical-validity, reproducibility, or evaluator hard gates.

title: Contextual usage memory
testable_consequence_1: Memory must store rendered use, Visual Syntax role, Activative function, composition relationships, recurrence intent, and VLM verdict.

title: Authority-aware retrieval
testable_consequence_1: Retrieval must filter knowledge by lifecycle status, authority class, compatibility, category, format, asset family, and current contract.

title: Hybrid multimodal retrieval
testable_consequence_1: Search must support lexical, semantic, image, composition, syntax-fingerprint, and graph signals.

title: Contradiction and exception coverage
testable_consequence_1: Context compilation must include applicable counterexamples, exceptions, superseding knowledge, and material contradictions.

title: Minimum Complete Context
testable_consequence_1: Retrieval must compile the smallest sufficient knowledge package and record included, excluded, and compressed resources.

title: Immutable runtime profiles
testable_consequence_1: Self-hosted jobs must use digest-pinned container images, ComfyUI versions, custom-node lockfiles, and system dependencies.

title: Capability-aware scheduling
testable_consequence_1: Jobs must route by required workflow, model, control, VRAM, queue, cache locality, cost, latency, and certified fallback.

title: Worker health and recovery
testable_consequence_1: Workers must expose health checks, job heartbeats, cancellation, timeout, artifact extraction, and replacement behavior.

title: Resource isolation
testable_consequence_1: Concurrent jobs must not mutate models, workflows, assets, caches, or execution state belonging to other jobs.

title: Local-cloud equivalence testing
testable_consequence_1: Certified local and cloud profiles must pass equivalent representative demand, output-contract, and recovery tests.

title: Policy-first controls
testable_consequence_1: The primary UI must expose budgets, priorities, experiment policies, exceptions, and authorization rather than requiring node-level production work.

title: Clear exceptional decisions
testable_consequence_1: Human-review packages must explain trigger, attempted repairs, preserved state, unresolved dimensions, choices, and consequences.

title: Visual evidence inspection
testable_consequence_1: Operators must inspect candidate comparisons, BBOX evidence, VLM evidence regions, composition renders, lineage, and recurrence contexts.

title: Accessible Control Tower
testable_consequence_1: Core supervisory surfaces must support keyboard navigation, readable status language, sufficient contrast, and non-color-only state cues.

title: No routine approval burden
testable_consequence_1: Passing routine jobs must complete without operator approval, manual candidate selection, prompt editing, or job restart.

title: Typed production nodes
testable_consequence_1: Every node must declare actor type, inputs, outputs, dependencies, validators, timeouts, retries, checkpoint, invalidation, and events.

title: Deterministic orchestration
testable_consequence_1: Routing, dependency resolution, contract validation, lifecycle transitions, and budget enforcement must be code-owned.

title: Infrastructure-quality separation
testable_consequence_1: Infrastructure retries and quality repairs must be independently classified, metered, and limited.

title: Dependency-safe parallelism
testable_consequence_1: Parallel candidates or preparation nodes require proven independence, bounded concurrency, cancellation, and merge semantics.

title: Workflow integration testing
testable_consequence_1: Tests must verify gating, resume, selective invalidation, service events, compute failover, and terminal-state correctness.

title: Upstream architecture preservation
testable_consequence_1: The product must not alter the validated Atomic Harness Builder architecture or shared Activative authority model.

title: Demand-owner authority
testable_consequence_1: Only the owning Content Harness may approve demand-level semantic, Activative, sequence, or composition amendments.

title: Registry promotion governance
testable_consequence_1: Capabilities and knowledge move through explicit experimental, benchmarked, shadow, limited, production, deprecated, and retired states.

title: Implementation authorization
testable_consequence_1: Architecture and implementation may proceed only through the formal readiness gates and receipts defined by the product.

title: Certified-scope honesty
testable_consequence_1: The product must clearly distinguish structurally represented, experimental, limited-production, and fully certified asset families and profiles.
